pending.body: Contract-marked prompt overrides can now invent `## Nearby work` IDs that review and final-review treat as valid `mapped_to_bead_id` owners. `create_project_from_bead_context` only shape-validates overrides ([src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:427-432]); later stages derive the planned-elsewhere allowlist directly from prompt text ([src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:191-195], [src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:467-530, 2382-2385], [src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:591-632]). A stale or hand-written override can therefore silently reroute findings to beads that were never graph-derived for this task.

Prompt-review guidance becomes self-contradictory on malformed contract-marked prompts that do not actually contain `## Nearby work`. When validation fails, `prompt_authoritative_section_titles()` falls back to the full current section list ([src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:197-210]), and `prompt_review_consumer_guidance_for_prompt()` then tells the refiner to preserve the graph-derived nearby-work body verbatim ([src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:284-300, 338-342]). The validator later rejects any refinement that adds `## Nearby work` when the original prompt lacked it ([src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:333-370]), so drift-recovery can churn on impossible instructions.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 23 -> Plan and Implement
